inputs.reasoning: Nested repetitions with alternation trigger complex stack manipulation in BitState::TrySearch. The outer loop iteration and inner repetition cause multiple Push operations and CheckAndLoop jumps.
Multiple alternations (kInstAlt) with arg switching between 0 and 1 exercise the switch(arg) cases and the CheckAndLoop goto mechanism extensively.
Named captures (kInstCapture) with arg==0 trigger capture register save/restore logic and the cap_ array access path.
Large counted repetitions compile into byte range matching with complex iteration, exercising the kInstByteRange case and the p++ advancement logic.
Deeply nested repetitions with multiple quantifiers cause extensive Push operations that may trigger GrowStack when njob_ >= maxjob_, exercising stack growth.
Negated character class with ranges exercises byte range matching (kInstByteRange) with the Matches check on various character values.
Non-greedy quantifier creates kInstAltMatch with greedy(prog_) returning false, exercising the non-greedy branch and the second Push path.
Multiple non-greedy quantifiers combined with byte ranges test the interaction of kInstAltMatch and kInstByteRange opcodes.
Nested alternation within repetition exercises the while(njob_ > 0) loop extensively and the interaction between kInstAlt and repetition operators.
Capture group with quantifier exercises kInstCapture with arg==0 path followed by byte range matching, testing the cap_ register logic.
